mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% as of 2020-03-15 indicates active easing cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods (FEDFUNDS < 1.0%) are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices adjust to lower earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% as of 2020-03-15, prior month likely 3.5% given COVID shock timing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window following unemployment shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to confirm demand destruction.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 483066M and HOUST at 1599K as of 2020-02-15, both likely to decline sharply in March 2020
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% simultaneously, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of forward returns on both proxies.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 103.0 signals supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade through the earnings season.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 103.0 and remains there for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.66 as of 2020-03-15, near threshold given COVID production shutdowns
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when INDPRO < 103.0 for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeding 1.0% signals rapid labor shedding that triggers equity repricing downward as investors anticipate lower consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151786K as of 2020-03-15, expected to fall sharply in April 2020 COVID report
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where PAYEMS declines > 1.0% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1.0% (indicating monetary easing), a month-over-month decline in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 0.2% signals deflationary pressure that increases real debt burdens and reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days despite monetary accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% and CPIAUCSL at 257.95 as of 2020-03-15
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting 20-day returns, or SPX produces positive returns in > 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) signals economic contraction that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices equity valuations downward in response to recession signals.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19221.97B as of 2020-10-15, Q1 2020 likely to show contraction
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) following quarter-over-quarter GDP declines, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a rise in unemployment (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3pp month-over-month signals a multi-sector demand collapse that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This three-factor interaction captures broad-based economic deterioration.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 1.5% AND RSAFS declines > 1.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:All three series expected to deteriorate sharply in March-April 2020 COVID period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where all three conditions are met simultaneously, or the three-way interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression.
